images.caption:
Figure 1:Geometric evidence influence patterns on KITTI and long-sequence scaling on VBR.
Prior streaming methods impose hard cutoffs, refresh discontinuities, heavy-tailed states, or spiky KV caches, leading to pose degradation, jitter, or collapse.
HorizonStream learns a bounded multi-scale kernel with anO​(1)O(1)recurrent geometric state and maintains stable ATE up to10​K10\mathrm{K}frames.

Figure 2:Visualization of long-range streaming 3D reconstruction across diverse scenes. Our method maintains stable trajectories and coherent geometry over sequences ranging from hundreds to thousands of frames in outdoor, indoor, and game environments.

Figure 3:Overview ofHorizonStream.
Given an RGB stream, the model causally processes the most recentWWframes. Geometric Local Attention handles local matching, Geometric Linear Attention propagates long-range geometry with anO​(1)O(1)recurrent geometric state, and Metric Readout Tokens recover stable scale and pose. An optional loop-closure module refines the trajectory.

Figure 4:Qualitative comparisonon long-sequence 3D reconstruction.
As sequence length grows, existing methods show pose degradation, drift, or collapse.
Lingbot-map exhibits progressively stronger pose jitter over longer rollouts, while HorizonStream maintains stable pose estimation.

Figure 5:Qualitative comparisonon 3D reconstruction. Left: trajectory. Right: 3D reconstruction. HorizonStream maintains stable geometry. Lingbot-map preserves trajectory direction but exhibits increasing jitter, causing point cloud overlap.

Figure 7:Memory and runtime scaling. HorizonStream keeps peak memory nearly constant and scales smoothly to10​K10\mathrm{K}frames, while competing methods require increasing memory or higher runtime on long sequences.
Figure 8:Training convergence under different attention mechanisms for cross-window propagation. Geometric Linear Attention with channel-wise gating converges faster and reaches a lower final loss.
Figure 9:Effect of loop closure on long sequences. Loop closure reduces ATE on sequences with revisited regions while maintaining performance elsewhere.

Figure 10:Failure cases on ultra-long sequences. Ground-truth trajectory (red), online prediction (blue), and loop-closure refined trajectory (green). Failures occur mainly in sequences with dense revisits or visually ambiguous regions.

Figure 11:Linear probing of frozen Geometric Linear Attention states. Ridge regressors trained on chunk-level state features predict four geometric error targets. Segment-scale log error is the most reliably predictable, suggesting that the recurrent state encodes measurable metric information. Other targets are less consistently captured by a linear probe.

Figure 11:Linear probing of frozen Geometric Linear Attention states. Ridge regressors trained on chunk-level state features predict four geometric error targets. Segment-scale log error is the most reliably predictable, suggesting that the recurrent state encodes measurable metric information. Other targets are less consistently captured by a linear probe.

Figure 12:Probe weight attribution by retention band (segment scale target). Weights are nearly uniform across short / medium / long, showing that scale signal is distributed rather than band-specific.
